assets.name: lecture_10_figure_01.png
assets.rationale: None of the candidate frames contain blackboard mathematics, diagrams, or lecture layout useful for note writing. They are all title-card variations, so this subtitle window should be discarded rather than kept as visual evidence.

assets.name: lecture_10_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: This frame shows the written equation in its most settled and unobstructed state within the window. The lecturer is no longer blocking the notation, the blue-board layout is clear, and the image preserves the local board context without adding distracting motion or gesture.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: component of the time derivative of the vector potential.
assets.caption_hint: electric field from the time derivative of the vector potential

assets.name: lecture_10_figure_03.png
assets.subtitle_excerpt: and it's just equal to the derivative of the Lagrangian
assets.caption_hint: and it's just equal to the derivative of the Lagrangian

assets.name: lecture_10_figure_04.png
assets.rationale: This frame is the clearest and least occluded view of the equation being discussed. The Lagrangian term at the top of the board is fully legible, while the lecturer’s hand is no longer covering the kinetic term as it does in the later candidates. It therefore gives the most useful board evidence for the moment when canonical momentum is identified through differentiation of the Lagrangian.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: The canonical momentum is the derivative of the Lagrangian
assets.caption_hint: particle Lagrangian used for canonical momentum

assets.name: lecture_10_figure_05.png
assets.subtitle_excerpt: The potential energy is the piece in the Lagrangian
assets.caption_hint: The potential energy is the piece in the Lagrangian

assets.name: lecture_10_figure_06.png
assets.subtitle_excerpt: we have plus potential energy in the Hamiltonian.
assets.caption_hint: we have plus potential energy in the Hamiltonian.
